title: Upon Moon.
content:
Moon is a usurer, whose gain,
    Seldom or never knows a wain,
    Only Moon's conscience, we confess,
    That ebbs from pity less and less.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "Upon Moon.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
